summary: M5-PLAYER-RESEARCH-001 is CLOSED only after PR #122 passed CI, merged into main, local main synchronized to origin/main, and active gameplay_designer/qa_reviewer child threads were closed. The integrated task records a minimal privacy-safe internal anonymous M5 player-research pilot and preserves its limitations. It does not pass M5, decide manual node battle, or adjudicate historical/cultural content.

tests_run.result: Quality, Web, and Browser Smoke passed in 1m47s; Windows Desktop Smoke Artifact passed in 1m1s.
Merged PR #122 and fast-forwarded local main to 8b6ef6542d2d1a8270618ec8bae2161eb94c2e9d.

tests_run.command: git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/main
tests_run.result: Local main and origin/main both resolve to 8b6ef6542d2d1a8270618ec8bae2161eb94c2e9d before closure branch edits.
Both active task child threads returned completed previous_status and were closed.

decisions: Mark M5-PLAYER-RESEARCH-001 CLOSED because accepted research evidence and QA review are integrated into origin/main via PR #122.
Keep M5 formal passage out of scope. M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 remains the formal passage gate.
Keep manual node battle decision out of scope. M5-MANUAL-NODE-BATTLE-DECISION-001 remains a separate Human Gate.
Carry duplicate React key warnings and minimal sample size as bounded follow-ups/limitations.
risks: P3 caveat: two internal anonymous sessions are minimal evidence and not recruited external player research.
P3 follow-up: duplicate React key warnings for repeated reason-code chips should be addressed in a bounded UI task.
P3 caveat: current save/load is client-session checkpoint behavior, not desktop file import/export UX.
requested_action: Commit closure, open closure PR, wait for CI, merge into main, then run taskctl ready. If the next READY task is a Human Gate, stop for human decision rather than auto-passing it.
